title: Metrics Glossary Version Control and Change Log
story: As a DataTools Pro user managing a Metrics Glossary, I want a change log that tracks all modifications to metrics (name, alias, definition, logic, topics, lines of business), so that I can audit who changed what and when, and eventually publish versioned snapshots of the glossary.
description:
The Metrics Glossary needed an audit trail for all transactional changes to metric records. This feature introduces a change log that records modifications to metric attributes and category assignments, with plans to later support publishing versioned glossary snapshots.

## Change Log Functionality

1. **Tracked changes** — modifications to metric name, alias, definition, logic, additions/removals of topics and lines of business, and report/dashboard associations are all logged.
2. **Log entries** include the action performed, the affected metric, old and new values, the user who made the change (first name and last name), and a timestamp.
3. **Filter by action** — a dropdown filter allows users to narrow the log by action type.
4. **User attribution** — every change records the individual user (not just the team account), supporting audit requirements even after a user is deactivated.

## Version Publishing (Future)

The long-term vision is to allow users to "publish" a version of the glossary at a point in time, with the change log tracking all modifications since the last published version — similar to Jira release notes.

## User Attribution Design Decision

Changes are logged per individual user rather than per team account. This supports audit scenarios where multiple team members (e.g., salesforce team, data team) collaborate on the glossary and accountability for individual changes is required. This also motivated a soft-delete approach for users to preserve audit trail integrity.
acceptance_criteria: All changes to metric name, alias, definition, and logic are logged
Additions and removals of topics and lines of business are logged
Report and dashboard association changes are logged
Each log entry records the action, metric, old/new values, user name, and timestamp
A dropdown filter allows filtering the change log by action type
User first and last name appears in a dedicated column
Changes are attributed to the individual user, not just the team account
Change log is scoped to the selected team account
ux_changes: New change log view within the Metrics Glossary showing a table of all modifications. Includes columns for action, metric, old/new values, user name, and timestamp. A dropdown filter narrows entries by action type.
middleware_changes: All metric mutation operations (create, update, delete, category assign/unassign, report/dashboard link) now write an audit log entry capturing the action, affected metric, old/new values, user identity, and timestamp. Log retrieval is scoped to the selected team account.
data_model_changes: New audit log table for metric changes. Stores action type, metric reference, old value, new value, user ID (individual, not team), and timestamp. Supports soft-delete of users to preserve referential integrity.
implementation_notes: The change log is scoped to the team account context — when an invited user selects a team account, they see the change log for that account. Individual user attribution required ensuring that the middleware passes the actual user ID (not the team/owner ID) when logging changes. This design supports future soft-delete of users to maintain audit trail integrity. Version publishing is planned as a follow-up feature.
comment_summary: Developer built an initial change log scoped to team accounts and deployed to staging. Product owner was enthusiastic and requested adding a filter dropdown by action type and a user name column. A design discussion ensued about whether to log changes per team account or per individual user. The decision was to log per individual user for accountability, which also motivated a soft-delete approach for users. Developer added the name column and filter dropdown.
